description_long: This object is a pair of pliers with a simple, minimalist design, featuring two elongated, slightly curved arms that meet at a central pivot. The arms are uniformly colored in a matte reddish hue, with no visible insulation or ergonomic shaping. The jaws are blunt and lack serrations or specialized gripping features, giving the tool a streamlined appearance. There are no visible markings, logos, or brand identifiers on the surface. The overall form is slender and lightweight, suggesting it is intended for light-duty gripping or manipulation tasks. The surface appears hard and smooth, typical of metal tools with a painted or powder-coated finish.
description: A minimalist, matte red pair of pliers with smooth, curved arms and blunt jaws, lacking visible branding or ergonomic features.
description_view_0: The pliers are seen from above, showing both arms splayed outward and the central pivot clearly visible.
description_view_1: A side view highlights the thickness and curvature of one arm, with the other arm extending away at an angle.
description_view_2: The opposite top-down angle reveals the symmetry of the arms and the blunt, untextured jaws.
description_view_3: A side profile displays the length and gentle curve of the arms, with the pivot point at the top.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, slightly curved, uninsulated metal handles with a uniform cross-section.
attribute_jaw_design: Blunt, flat jaws with no serrations, notches, or wire-cutting features; basic gripping surfaces.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed single pivot joint, non-adjustable, allowing standard opening and closing motion.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth, untextured metal surface with no visible grip pattern or rubberized coating.
